search_multi.highlightHint: Left-click for next; Right-click for previous
search_multi.explain: Find files matching ANY of the following conditions
search_multi.operator.gte: is greater than or equal to
search_multi.operator.lte: is less than or equal to
search_multi.scope.name: File Name (No Extension)
search_multi.scope.head: Heading
search_multi.modal.hintDetail.syntax: Combine multiple conditions to precisely locate files. A condition consists of a Scope, an Operator, and an Operand. E.g., {{eg}} (Find files larger than 2KB).
search_multi.modal.hintDetail.combineCond.and: Use AND to require all conditions to be met. E.g., {{eg}}
search_multi.modal.hintDetail.combineCond.or: Use OR if only one of the conditions needs to be met. E.g., {{eg}}
search_multi.modal.hintDetail.combineCond.not: Add NOT to exclude a condition. E.g., {{eg}}
search_multi.modal.hintDetail.combineCond.parentheses: Use parentheses to group conditions. E.g., {{eg}}
search_multi.modal.hintDetail.operator.colon: Contains match or Regular Expression match. Used for text content, indicating that the search scope contains or regular matches the search value
search_multi.modal.hintDetail.operator.equal: Strict equality / inequality. Used for exact comparison of text, numerical, or boolean values
search_multi.modal.hintDetail.operator.compare: Numerical comparison. Used for numerical attributes, such as file size or time
search_multi.modal.hintDetail.operand.quotes: Text enclosed in double quotes signifies an exact match for the entire phrase. E.g., {{eg}}
search_multi.modal.hintDetail.operand.regex: Text enclosed in slashes indicates the use of a JavaScript-style regular expression for advanced pattern matching. E.g., {{eg}}
search_multi.modal.hintDetail.syntacticSugar: You can omit the Scope and Operator. The default scope is {{scope}} (path or content), and the default operator is {{operator}} (contains). Typing {{shortCond}} is exactly the same as typing {{longCond}}.
search_multi.modal.example.desc6: Matches the regex \bsour\b (whole word match for sour), and was modified before 2024-05-16
search_multi.modal.example.desc7: YAML Front Matter contains 'dev', OR a heading is exactly 'plugin', OR bold text contains 'MIT'
search_multi.modal.example.desc8: File is larger than 10KB AND (has over 1000 lines OR contains an image)
search_multi.modal.example.desc9: Path excludes 'warn' and 'err', AND extension is not 'md'
search_multi.modal.example.desc10: Table header contains 'k8s', H2 contains 'prometheus', and a code block contains 'kubectl apply'
search_multi.$label.STOP_SEARCHING_ON_HIDING: Stop Searching When Window is Hidden
search_multi.$label.BACKSPACE_TO_HIDE: Hide Window on Empty and Backspace

myopic_defocus.demoExplain:
Simulates 0.23D Myopic Defocus via Longitudinal Chromatic Aberration (LCA), guiding the focal plane to fall in front of the retina.
Mechanism: Eyes refract wavelengths differently: Blue light focuses in front, Green on the retina, and Red behind. This extension algorithmically shifts pixel colors to mimic this optical variance, creating a protective defocus signal that relieves accommodation stress.
myopic_defocus.$explain.enableMyopicDefocus: Simulates myopic defocus to relieve refractive stress. Note: This causes slight color fringing on text edges and may impact rendering performance

auto_number.$label.LAYOUTS.name: Name
auto_number.$hintDetail.layoutSyntax: Format uses 'Prefix{Name:Style}Suffix'. Prefix/Suffix can be any text. {} is the placeholder. 'Name' targets the object, and 'Style' defines the counter type. You can freely mix text and multiple counters. E.g., 'Fig {i:d}' produces 'Fig 1', 'Fig 2'.
auto_number.$hintDetail.counterNames: c1~c6 (Content H1~H6), o1~o6 (Outline H1~H6), t1~t6 (TOC H1~H6), t (Tables), i (Images), and f (Fenced Code).
auto_number.$hintDetail.counterStyles: d (Decimal, default), dlz (Decimal Leading Zero), lr (Lower Roman), ur (Upper Roman), la (Lower Alpha), ua (Upper Alpha), lg (Lower Greek), hs (Heavenly Stem), eb (Earthly Branch), cjk (CJK Ideographs), di (Disc), ci (Circle), sq (Square).

mouse_gestures.$label.GESTURES.name: Name
mouse_gestures.$tooltip.STRATEGY: Adaptive: Prioritizes 8-way recognition, but falls back to 4-way for complex trajectories to improve accuracy
mouse_gestures.$tooltip.HYSTERESIS: Increases turn-detection threshold to filter out micro-jitters at boundary angles
mouse_gestures.$tooltip.START_TIMEOUT: If the pointer doesn't move within this time after pressing the trigger, it's treated as a normal click. Set to 0 to disable
mouse_gestures.$tooltip.IDLE_TIMEOUT: If the pointer pauses longer than this duration mid-gesture, the gesture aborts. Set to 0 to disable
mouse_gestures.$tooltip.COOLDOWN: Adds a delay after a gesture to prevent accidental rapid-fire triggers
mouse_gestures.$tooltip.MACRO_RADIUS: Minimum pixel distance required to register a new direction. Larger values reduce accidental triggers but require wider hand movements
mouse_gestures.$tooltip.TAIL_RADIUS: Threshold used to recognize the final, brief trajectory segment upon release. Usually smaller than 'Macro Radius' to maintain end-motion sensitivity
